description: Immerse your supporters in the spirit of activism and conservation with our specially designed email for non-profit and fundraising initiatives. This template is expertly crafted to inspire action and awareness around the critical need to protect our oceans. With its compelling visuals and persuasive text, it serves as a powerful tool for organizations striving to make a meaningful impact on World Ocean Day. It's more than just an email; it's a call to action for individuals to contribute to ocean conservation efforts. Whether you're soliciting donations, encouraging volunteer work, or simply spreading the word about the importance of our oceans, this email template is your key to effective fundraising and awareness campaigns.
short_description: Engage your supporters with our World Ocean Day Email template. Perfect for non-profit and fundraising initiatives, it inspires action for ocean conservation. Ideal for donations, volunteer work, or spreading awareness. Your key to effective campaigns.

designer.description: I am a Chicago-based graphic designer. I specialize in working with natural brands. I can work with you remotely or in-person as your creative partner to tackle branding and marketing projects of any format, digital or print. Let's make something amazing together.

html_data:
GET INVOLVED

OCEANS OF

PLASTIC

Rescue the Seas

Our planet’s oceans produce half of our oxygen, feed a billion people, and are home to some of the most magnificent species on the planet. Climate change, pollution, and damaging industries, on the other hand, put them in peril now more than ever. To fix the issue, we must act immediately. 

 

HELP US REACH 1,000,000 SIGNATURES

Join the campaign →
 
 

Over 750,000 people have joined the campaign.

 

The microplastics emergency

 

The most frequent sort of marine trash found in our oceans and Great Lakes is plastic. Microplastics are little plastics with a length of less than five millimeters. That’s about the size of a sesame seed.

Learn More

Preserving our blue planet

Scientists propose a new strategy to rescue our oceans: create a worldwide network of ocean sanctuaries that would keep harmful industries out of millions of square kilometers. But to get world governments on board, we need your help to show why ocean conservation is so important.

Sign the petition

“I saw destruction first-hand”

Listen to our podcast with diver Jake Mills, as he describes the dangers plastics pose to marine ecosystems and how we can work together to ensure a biodiverse, thriving ocean for future generations. 

Listen Now
 
 

Spread the news. Together, we can protect

at least 30% of our blue planet by 2030

#30x30

 
 
NEWS | DONATE | SUBSCRIBE

If you prefer not to receive marketing emails form this list, click here to unsubscribe.
